# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

_format_version: "2.1"
plugins:
  - name: cors
    service_id: null
    route_id: null
    consumer_id: null
    enabled: true
    config:
      origins: ["http://example.com", "https://api.example.com"]
      methods: ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"]
      headers: ["Authorization", "Content-Type", "Accept"]
      exposed_headers: ["X-Custom-Header"]
      max_age: 86400
      credentials: true
    global: true

  - name: key-auth
    service_id: null
    route_id: null
    consumer_id: null
    enabled: true
    config:
      key_names: ["apikey"]
    global: false

  - name: rate-limiting
    service_id: null
    route_id: null
    consumer_id: null
    enabled: true
    config:
      second: 5
      hour: 1000
      policy: local
    global: false

services:
  - name: example-service
    url: http://example-backend.com
    plugins:
      - name: key-auth
        consumer_id: null
        enabled: true
        config:
          key_names: ["service-key"]
      - name: rate-limiting
        consumer_id: null
        enabled: true
        config:
          second: 10
          minute: 200

routes:
  - name: example-route
    paths: ["/api/v1/*"]
    service:
      name: example-service
    plugins:
      - name: cors
        consumer_id: null
        enabled: true
        config:
          origins: ["http://app.example.com"]
          methods: ["GET", "POST"]
